title: Breitmann am Rhein - Cologne
content:
آ آ آ آ How wunderschï؟½n das Vaterland
آ آ آ آ In audumn-life abbears;
آ آ آ آ Vot rainpows gild ids vallies crand,
آ آ آ آ Ven seen troo vallin tears.
آ آ آ آ Und VON Iï؟½ll creet mit sang und klang,
آ آ آ آ Und drown in goldnen wein;
آ آ آ آ Old Deutschlandï؟½s cot her sohn again:
آ آ آ آ Hans Breitmannï؟½s on der Rhein.

آ آ آ آ Und doughts ish schwell dat mighdy heart,
آ آ آ آ Too awfool for make known;
آ آ آ آ Ven dey shunt him from de railroat car
آ آ آ آ Und tropped him in Cologne.
آ آ آ آ De holy towers of de dome
آ آ آ آ Cleam, twilicht-veiled, afar;
آ آ آ آ Und like some lonely bilgrimï؟½s pipe,
آ آ آ آ Dim shines de efenin star.

آ آ آ آ Hans look to find his baggage check,
آ آ آ آ Und see dat all ish shdraighdts,
آ آ آ آ Denn toorn him to de city toors,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Mein nadife landï؟½wie gehts?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Boot datï؟½s vot all who read may runï؟½
آ آ آ آ Fool blainly armies write;
آ آ آ آ Idï؟½s ofer all half Shermany,
آ آ آ آ Set down in Black and White.

آ آ آ آ Oh, Black and White! O Weiss and Schwarz!
آ آ آ آ Vot dings ish dis to see?
آ آ آ آ I vonder vot in future years
آ آ آ آ Your mission ish to pe?
آ آ آ آ Also in crate America
آ آ آ آ We had soosh colors too!
آ آ آ آ Die Fï؟½rbï؟½ sind mir nicht unbekanntï؟½
آ آ آ آ Idï؟½s shoost tout comme chez nous.

آ آ آ آ Next tay to de Cathedral
آ آ آ آ He vent de dings to view,
آ آ آ آ Und found it shoost drei thaler cost
آ آ آ آ To see de sighds all troo.
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Idï؟½s tear,ï؟½ said Hans; ï؟½boot go ahet,
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½fe cot de cash all right;
آ آ آ آ Boot idï؟½s queer datï؟½s only Protestands
آ آ آ آ Vot mosdly see de sighdt!

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Im Mittelalter I hafe read
آ آ آ آ De shoorsh vas alvays sureï؟½
آ آ آ آ An open bicdure gallerie,
آ آ آ آ Und book for all de poor.
آ آ آ آ Boot now de dings is so arrange
آ آ آ آ No poor volk can get in;
آ آ آ آ We Yankees und de Englisch are
آ آ آ آ Pout all ash shbends de tin.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½I shmiles like Mephistopheles
آ آ آ آ In shoorshes ven I see
آ آ آ آ Poor Catholics vollerin round apout
آ آ آ آ To shdeal a sighdtï؟½troo ME!
آ آ آ آ Dey peep und creep roundt chapel gates,
آ آ آ آ Boot soon kits trofe afay,
آ آ آ آ Dey gross demselfs, und make a brayerï؟½
آ آ آ آ Boot den dey cannot bay!

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Dese Deutsche sacrisdans might learn
آ آ آ آ More goot in Italy,
آ آ آ آ Where beoples bays shoost half de brice,
آ آ آ آ For ten dimes more to see,
آ آ آ آ De volk vot dink I shbeak sefere
آ آ آ آ Apout dese Kï؟½ster vays,
آ آ آ آ May read vot Mr. Bï؟½deker
آ آ آ آ In his Belgine Hand Buch says.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Und valkin oop und town de down
آ آ آ آ Von ding vas shdill de same:
آ آ آ آ Shoost ash of oldt he saw de shpread
آ آ آ آ Of Jean Farinaï؟½s name.
آ آ آ آ He find it nortï؟½, he find it soutï؟½,
آ آ آ آ He find it eferyvhere;
آ آ آ آ Dere vas no house in all Cologne
آ آ آ آ Boot J.M.F. vas dere.

آ آ آ آ De best Cologne in all Cologne
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll shwear for cerdain sure,
آ آ آ آ Ish maket in de Jï؟½lichsplatz
آ آ آ آ Und dat at Numero Four.
آ آ آ آ Boot of dis Cologne in Jï؟½lichsplatz
آ آ آ آ Let dis pe understood,
آ آ آ آ Dat some of id ish foorst-rate pad,
آ آ آ آ Vhile some is foorst-rate good.

آ آ آ آ Boot von ding drafellers moost opserve,
آ آ آ آ Dis treadful trut I dells,
آ آ آ آ Fast ash dis Farinaceous crowd
آ آ آ آ So vast hafe grown the schmellsï؟½
آ آ آ آ Dose awfool schmells in gassï؟½ und strassï؟½
آ آ آ آ Vitch mofe crate Coleridge squalm:
آ آ آ آ If so he wrote, vot vouldt he write
آ آ آ آ Apout dem now, py tam?

آ آ آ آ Of all de schmells I efer schmelt,
آ آ آ آ Py gutter, sink, or well,
آ آ آ آ At efery gorner of Cologne
آ آ آ آ Dereï؟½s von can peat dat schmell.
آ آ آ آ Vhen dere you go youï؟½ll find it so,
آ آ آ آ Donï؟½t dake de ding on troost;
آ آ آ آ De meanest skunk in Yankee land
آ آ آ آ Vould die dere of disgoost.

آ آ آ آ Boot noding dinked der Breitmann
آ آ آ آ Of schmutz or idle schein,
آ آ آ آ Vhen he sat in Abendï؟½mmerung
آ آ آ آ Und looket owd on der Rhein
آ آ آ آ Im goldnen gleamï؟½vhile pealin far
آ آ آ آ Rang shlow, shveet kloster bells,
آ آ آ آ Und in de dim, plue peaudiful,
آ آ آ آ Rose distant Drachenfels.

آ آ آ آ Dey trinket lieb Liebfrauenmilch
آ آ آ آ So pure ash vomanï؟½s trutï؟½;
آ آ آ آ De singed de songs of Shermany,
آ آ آ آ De songs of Breitmannï؟½s youtï؟½.
آ آ آ آ De songs mit tears of vanished years,
آ آ آ آ Made peaudiful in wein.
آ آ آ آ Dus endet out de firster tay
آ آ آ آ Of Breitmann on der Rhein.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles G. Leland represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Charles G. Leland's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
